summary: Gruber highlights a humorous April Fools' Day promotion from Bare Bones Software offering a $250,000 BBEdit license that includes hand delivery by an employee and a year of personal service. The joke offer includes absurd perks like reading the manual aloud and ripping the cellophane off the CD. Gruber notes the additional requirement of providing microbrewed beer, with a surcharge for mass-market beer. He adds his own suggestion to stock quality coffee, linking to Peet's and Torreo, showing his familiarity with the Bare Bones team's tastes.
key_points: Bare Bones Software posted a comedic $250,000 BBEdit license offer as an April Fools' joke
The offer parodies enterprise software pricing with absurd personal service perks
The beer and coffee details reflect the indie Mac developer culture Gruber is embedded in
money_quotes: Now available is the option to purchase a single user license of BBEdit with hand delivery by a Bare Bones Software employee and one full year of personal service
a refrigerator full of microbrewed beer (20% surcharge for mass-market or 'lite' beer)
key_insight: Bare Bones Software's April Fools' joke lampoons enterprise software pricing while showcasing the personality and humor of the indie Mac developer community.
